_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron)
_meta.note: VA local batch — evidence-finalize Norfolk Mayor Kenny Alexander + Vice Mayor Martin Thomas Jr (Ward 1) + Mamie Johnson (Ward 3) + John Paige (Ward 4) + Jeremy McGee (Superward 6). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Mayor Alexander — long-serving D, ordained Christian minister since 1980s — TRUE public_justice q0 on 'My council and I, we are not defunding police. We are funding police' + +43 officers + largest sheriff's academy in a decade (HARD anti-defund); FALSE christian_liberty q0 on his explicit anti-public-profession stance ('never cross that line, then you are no better than the Taliban'); soft FALSE biblical_marriage q4 on Sept 2024 Harris-Walz endorsement at a Norfolk LGBTQ-community rally (no signed Pride proclamation located). Three confirmed cells; 26 honestly null. (2) Vice Mayor Martin Thomas Jr — Ward 1 personal-injury attorney, Northam-appointed VA College Building Authority board, re-elected Vice Mayor 1/7/2025 — pro-police rhetoric found ('Norfolk must have a world-class police force'; championed first-responder pay increases) but LEFT NULL per Doyle-precedent sparse-data discipline (single-vector campaign rhetoric without independent endorsement vector; 100/100 from a 1-cell denominator would be misleading). roster_verified; 30 honestly null. (3) Mamie Johnson — Ward 3, 14-yr NPS teacher, endorsed Jay Jones D for AG — FALSE public_justice q0 ('Is the police department the best resource to address that? No' — explicitly supports reallocating police budget to non-police mental-health crisis team) + FALSE public_justice q3 (embraces the public-health framing of crime); 28 honestly null. (4) John Paige — Ward 4, US Air Force veteran, bail bondsman, Catholic (Basilica of St. Mary), I — FALSE public_justice q3 on explicit public-health framing of gun violence ('Gun violence is a public safety concern as well as a public health concern'); 29 honestly null. Fix party Unknown→I; running for re-election Nov 2026 (qualified 3/10/2026, no challenger as of 5/20/2026). (5) Jeremy McGee — Superward 6 (citywide), freshman sworn 1/1/2025, Radiant Digital CEO + Four Eleven York co-owner, ran with Northam/Rouse/Williams Graves/Hernandez/Jay Jones endorsement slate — pro-police-staffing campaign rhetoric found + IAFF Local 68 firefighter (not police) union endorsement, but LEFT NULL per Doyle-precedent sparse-data discipline (single-vector campaign-platform self-claim; 100/100 from a 1-cell denominator would mis-inflate a freshman with no other recorded positions). roster_verified; 30 honestly null. Fix party Unknown→I. ROSTER FIXES applied via set blocks. ROSTER VERIFIED for all 5: Norfolk City Council seats are officially nonpartisan; Alexander term 2025-2028 (re-elected 2024 w/ 59.3%), Thomas/Johnson/Paige term 2023-2026 (on Nov 2026 ballot), McGee term 2025-2028 (not on 2026 ballot). NORFOLK FIREARMS ORDINANCE NOTE: the 2020 Norfolk firearms-in-public-buildings ordinance was PULLED from the 8/25/2020 agenda by the City Attorney before any roll-call and never returned — unlike Newport News which expanded its ban in 2024, Norfolk has NO firearms-buildings ordinance on the books. No 2A FALSE for any Norfolk councilmember on that basis. NORFOLK PRIDE NOTE: Norfolk's official Pride Month page is run by city administration ('Team Norfolk Today'), not a council roll-call proclamation — only the Mayor's documented LGBTQ-rally appearance is scoreable (soft) on Alexander.

profile.religion: Christian (licensed and ordained Minister since the 1980s, nondenominational, non-pastoring; speaks at 'churches, temples, mosques, and synagogues' per WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile). Explicitly compartmentalizes faith from governance.
profile.education: Lake Taylor HS (1985); A.S. Mortuary Science (Brightpoint CC, formerly John Tyler CC); B.A. Political Science (Old Dominion); M.A. Diplomacy (Norwich); Ph.D. Leadership and Change (Antioch, 2019)
profile.background: Born and raised in Norfolk. President, Metropolitan Funeral Service. Vice Chancellor for Strategic Partnerships, Virginia Community College System. VA House of Delegates 89th District 2002-2012. VA Senate 5th District 2012-2016. Virginia Legislative Black Caucus chair (2008). 25th and first Black Mayor of Norfolk (since 7/1/2016); re-elected 11/5/2024 with 59.3% (def. Thomas Leeman Jr. 33.4%, Giovanni Dolmo 6.6%). Current term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Norfolk City Council mayoral seat is officially nonpartisan but Alexander is a lifelong Democrat; endorsed Harris-Walz at a Norfolk 'Out for Harris' rally Sept 2024.

kenny-alexander.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Norfolk (officially nonpartisan; D-aligned per lifelong party affiliation + 2024 Harris-Walz Norfolk LGBTQ rally appearance). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: Direct quote in WTKR coverage of gun-violence response — 'My council and I, we are not defunding police. We are funding police.' Plus 13NewsNow (June 2020) reporting Alexander 'do[es] not support reducing the police department's budget at all.' State of the City messaging across 2024-2026 credits adding 43 new sworn officers + 'largest academy class of sheriff's deputies in a decade' for the 20% violent-crime drop. Multi-year affirmative pro-police pattern clears the high bar — this is exactly the kind of D-aligned official who EARNS the TRUE on the police-backing cell per the rubric's principle of giving credit where earned. HARD adverse christian_liberty q0 evidence: in WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile, Alexander explicitly framed public profession of his Christian conviction as off-limits in governance — 'You have to be careful that you understand that line, and never cross that line, because then you are no better than the Taliban.' He is a Christian minister (licensed and ordained since the 1980s, nondenominational) who deliberately keeps Christianity out of policy; that is documented evidence AGAINST the rubric's affirmative-public-profession standard. SOFT adverse biblical_marriage q4 evidence: WHRO 9/4/2024 — Alexander gave remarks endorsing Harris-Walz at 'Out for Harris' LGBTQ-community rally at MJ's Tavern in Norfolk. Explicit alliance with LGBTQ political activism. (Not scored as HARD because I did not locate a signed Pride proclamation from the Mayor's office — the City's Pride Month communications appear to be administrative/PR, not mayoral proclamations.) NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement, no PP endorsement, no SBA endorsement), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — mayor doesn't sit on NPS school board; no comment located on the 2023 NPS 'Get Real' adoption or any school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI issue), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4, economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — local budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (his state-senate roll calls on SB 731/SB 719 not retrievable in this pass), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4 (no documented friction with Richmond on lockdowns/mandates/ESG/unconstitutional directives — generally cooperative posture), border_immigration q1/q2 (no statement; Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron operates an ICE IGSA at the city jail but that is sheriff function, not mayoral), self_defense q0/q1 (NO Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance on the books — the 2020 ordinance was pulled by the City Attorney before any roll-call and never returned; cannot score Alexander adverse on that basis). Counter-archetype context: D mayor + ordained Christian minister + affirmative anti-defund pro-police record + explicit church-state separation posture. Verification note: the 'Apostolic bishop' framing sometimes used did NOT verify — he is listed as 'licensed and ordained Minister' per WTKR; not on Norfolk Apostolic Church staff (Sr. Pastor is Michael Blankenship).

0.note: Ordained Christian minister since the 1980s, but explicitly bars public profession of Christ from governance: 'You have to be careful that you understand that line, and never cross that line, because then you are no better than the Taliban.' Documented stance AGAINST the rubric's affirmative-public-profession standard.

4.note: Alexander appeared as headlining political endorser at the Sept 2024 'Out for Harris' LGBTQ-community rally at MJ's Tavern, Norfolk — explicit alliance with LGBTQ political activism (soft adverse; no signed Pride proclamation located).

0.note: Explicit anti-defund advocacy in WTKR gun-violence response: 'My council and I, we are not defunding police. We are funding police.' 13NewsNow June 2020: Alexander does not support reducing the police department's budget at all. State of the City: added 43 new officers and credited 'largest sheriff's academy in a decade' for the 20% violent-crime drop. Multi-year affirmative pro-police advocacy clears the high bar.

profile.education: B.A. Public & Urban Affairs, Virginia Tech (2002); J.D., University of Richmond School of Law (2006)
profile.background: Partner at Decker Law Firm (Norfolk personal-injury practice) since 2006. Lives in Wards Corner with wife Maggie and two sons. Norfolk City Council, Ward 1 (officially nonpartisan), first elected 8/23/2016, re-elected 11/8/2022 with 79.3% over Padraig-Eoin Leigh Dalrymple. Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot). Vice Mayor of Norfolk since July 2018, most recently re-elected Vice Mayor by unanimous council roll-call 1/7/2025 through 12/31/2026. Northam-appointed member, Virginia College Building Authority Board; Hampton Roads Transportation Planning Organization; Greater Wards Corner Task Force; Peter Decker Children's Charity Board.

martin-thomas-jr.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council Ward 1 (officially nonpartisan) + Vice Mayor; term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot); re-elected Vice Mayor 1/7/2025. D-aligned soft signals only (Northam-appointed VA College Building Authority Board seat; no public dissent from council mainstream under Mayor Alexander D); no formal party label — set to 'I'. PRO-POLICE RHETORIC FOUND but LEFT NULL on public_justice q0 per the SKILL's sparse-data-trap discipline: his 2022 candidate profile coverage has Thomas naming public safety his top priority, calling for a 'world-class police force,' and as councilman 'successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees.' That is single-vector campaign-and-budget framing without an independent endorsement vector (no police-union endorsement located, no quoted anti-defund denunciation) — same shape as the Doyle precedent (refinements/natl-local-VA-2026-06-02-1441.json), where the SKILL discipline kept the only police-related cell null to avoid a misleading 100/100 score from a 1-cell denominator. Marked roster_verified accordingly. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement), biblical_marriage (any cell — no quoted statement on marriage, biological sex, or Pride proclamations; Norfolk's Pride Month observance is administrative city PR, no council roll-call located), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — his only schools-adjacent action is co-leading a 2025 council push to force NPS to consolidate 10 schools, which is fiscal/efficiency, not parental-rights/CRT-SOGI/choice), christian_liberty (any cell — no church affiliation surfaced, no profession of Christ), economic_stewardship q2 (the schools-consolidation resolution is local efficiency, not federal-style BBA framing), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (his pro-prosecution remarks about stolen-gun offenders being 'arrested, prosecuted appropriately and efficiently, and sent to prison' don't name cashless bail or Soros-DA policy — soft, not hard), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — Northam-appointed board seat is a cooperative posture, not a specific overreach vote), border_immigration (any cell — Norfolk on DHS 2025 sanctuary list, but Thomas not quoted in any coverage either way), self_defense q0/q1 (the 2020 Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance was pulled before any roll-call and never returned — Thomas has no recorded vote on it; the 2A sanctuary petition failed signatures so there was no vote on it either). Counter-archetype context: quiet, ward-focused, downtown-attorney council technocrat with a Northam state-board appointment; thin culture-war footprint by design.

profile.education: Maury High School (Norfolk, 1984; Senior Class President); B.A. Education, Norfolk State University
profile.background: Norfolk native. 14 years as a teacher with Norfolk Public Schools; 6 years as Instructional Specialist; recently math/reading teacher specialist with Virginia Beach Public Schools. Husband: William H. Johnson. Norfolk City Council Ward 3 (officially nonpartisan), first elected 5/6/2014 (sworn 7/1/2014); re-elected 11/8/2022 with 96.5% (effectively unopposed, only write-ins). Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot). Board service: Virginia First Cities Coalition (immediate past chair); National Black Caucus of Local Elected Officials; Elizabeth River Project; Hampton Roads Workforce Council; Community Health Assessment / Community Health Plan; former Hampton Roads Planning District Commission; former co-chair Mayor's Lifelong Learning Commission. D-aligned: endorsed Jay Jones (D) for Virginia Attorney General; listed in Progressive Voters Guide 2022.

mamie-johnson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council Ward 3 (officially nonpartisan, D-aligned by endorsement pattern). Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot; effectively unopposed in 2022 at 96.5%). HARD adverse public_justice q0 evidence: in the Yahoo News / Virginian-Pilot story 'Defund police? Here's why Norfolk's chief says yes,' Johnson asked rhetorically about police responding to mental-health calls and said 'Is the police department the best resource to address that? No,' stating she wanted Norfolk to create a non-police mental-health crisis team and would support reallocating budget funds to establish it. That is the textbook soft-defund framing the rubric scores as FALSE on the affirmative-police-backing cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: the same reallocate-to-mental-health-crisis-team posture directly embraces the public-health framing for violent-crime response that q3 scores against. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; her Jay Jones endorsement is soft D-alignment signal, not personal abolition stance), biblical_marriage (any cell — no roll-call vote on a Pride proclamation located; Norfolk's Pride Month observance is administrative, no council resolution), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — 14-yr NPS teacher background is structural public-school bias but not a HARD anti-choice statement; no public position on parental notification, CRT, SOGI), christian_liberty (any cell — no church affiliation surfaced, no faith profession), economic_stewardship q2 (routine local budget votes not affirmative evidence per rubric), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q4, refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell — Norfolk Jail operates an ICE IGSA but that is sheriff function, not council vote; no Johnson statement located either way), self_defense q0/q1 (the 2020 Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance was pulled before any roll-call and never returned; the 2A sanctuary petition failed signatures; Johnson has no recorded firearms vote). Counter-archetype context: career NPS educator + D-aligned + reallocate-from-police on mental-health calls — moderate progressive ward member.

0.note: In Yahoo/Virginian-Pilot 'Defund police? Here's why Norfolk's chief says yes' coverage, Johnson asked of police responding to mental-health calls: 'Is the police department the best resource to address that? No.' She stated she would like the city to create a non-police mental-health crisis team and would support reallocating budget funds from police to establish it. Textbook soft-defund framing — adverse to the affirmative-police-backing standard.

3.note: Same source: the reallocate-from-police-to-mental-health-crisis-team posture directly embraces the public-health framing for police response that the rubric scores against.

profile.religion: Catholic (member with wife Djuna of the Basilica of St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception, Norfolk parish of the Diocese of Richmond, per official city bio)
profile.education: Norfolk State University alumnus; M.S. Management, Troy University (1998)
profile.background: Norfolk native (grew up in Huntersville/Norview/Ballantine neighborhoods). U.S. Air Force veteran (Operation Desert Shield/Desert Storm). Bail bondsman (small-business owner, 20+ years). Former adjunct professor at Norfolk State University (School of Business; School of Hospitality & Tourism Management). Personal story: dropped out after 10th grade, arrested at 17 and spent time in juvenile detention, earned GED, joined Air Force Reserve, went back to college. Great-great grandson of Rev. Richard G.L. Paige (early Black member of the Virginia General Assembly, 1871). Married 25+ years to Djuna; Kappa Alpha Psi (1992); board member Eggleston Services and Revive Community of Virginia; living organ donor. Norfolk City Council Ward 4 (officially nonpartisan; ran and serves as Independent), first elected 11/8/2022 with ~54% over Phillip Hawkins Jr (I) and Erik Barrett (D, 7.4%) — succeeded 30-year incumbent Paul Riddick. Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot). Qualified for re-election 3/10/2026; per the Norfolk Office of Elections 'List of Qualified Local Candidates' (5/20/2026), no challenger has yet qualified for Ward 4 (filing deadline 6/16/2026).

john-paige.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council Ward 4 (officially nonpartisan; ran as Independent per VPAP). Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (ON Nov 2026 ballot); qualified for re-election 3/10/2026 and currently unopposed as of 5/20/2026 — set candidacy_status to general_candidate. Party Unknown→I. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: in WAVY candidate profile and WTKR rally coverage, Paige explicitly stated 'Gun violence is a public safety concern as well as a public health concern,' calling for 'real and inclusive dialogue with the community' including 'those who have experienced violence, those who have practiced violence, and those who have not' and 'taking those that were once violent and indoctrinating them into being community leaders and community partners.' Literal embrace of the public-health framing the rubric scores against. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Catholic parish membership is private identity, not affirmative civic position — do not infer), biblical_marriage (any cell — no Pride proclamation roll-call located), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — his only school-related quoted concern was the 2025 consolidation debate's age-mixing issue, which is child-safeguarding not CRT/SOGI/choice), christian_liberty (any cell — Catholic identity is not a public civic profession of Christ; no public-square symbol or prayer advocacy located), economic_stewardship q2 (his June 2023 NO vote on the FY2024 CIP was equity/redistribution framing, NOT fiscal restraint or BBA — 'inequity of the money being distributed' — so does not score TRUE), election_integrity (any cell — Norfolk uses paper ballots with optical scanners by state mandate; no personal Paige statement), public_justice q0 (his frame is partnership-plus-redesign with 'partner with law enforcement' rhetoric AND calling for 'a redesign of public safety' — does not clear the affirmative anti-defund high bar; bondsman business interest is structural, not stated advocacy), public_justice q1/q2/q4 (no statements located), refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration (any cell — Norfolk on the DHS 2025 sanctuary list but Paige not quoted), self_defense q0/q1 (no firearms ordinance vote during his tenure — the 2020 buildings-ban was pre-Paige and pulled before vote; his public-health framing of gun violence is already scored on q3 and the rubric should not double-score). Counter-archetype context: Catholic + USAF veteran + bail bondsman + Republican-sounding biography — but progressive-coded equity/public-health framing on guns and budget.

3.note: Explicitly embraced the public-health framing of violent crime: 'Gun violence is a public safety concern as well as a public health concern' — and called for 'taking those that were once violent and indoctrinating them into being community leaders and community partners' rather than enforcement-first response. Literal rubric-adverse framing.

profile.education: B.S. Computer Engineering, University of South Carolina
profile.background: Originally from small-town South Carolina; Norfolk 'by choice.' CEO of Radiant Digital (digital media firm, ~50 employees). Co-owner with wife Malia of Four Eleven York boutique inn and restaurant in Norfolk. Past President, Ghent Neighborhood League. Prior city service: Norfolk Planning Commission; Architectural Review Board; VisitNorfolk Board. Currently co-chairs Mayor's Housing Commission; serves on Hampton Roads Planning District Commission, Erosion Advisory Committee, Coastal Management Review Board. Norfolk City Council Superward 6 (citywide-elected, officially nonpartisan), elected 11/5/2024 with ~69% over Jeremy Cyril Mason for the open seat vacated by term-limited Andria McClellan. Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Officially nonpartisan; D-aligned by endorsement slate (former Gov. Ralph Northam, VA Sens. Aaron Rouse + Angelia Williams Graves, Del. Phil Hernandez, former Del. Jay Jones, Education Association of Norfolk PAC, Progressive Voters Guide). Party set to 'I'.

jeremy-mcgee.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council Superward 6 (citywide; officially nonpartisan, D-aligned per endorsement slate). Freshman sworn 1/1/2025; term to 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Party Unknown→I. PRO-POLICE CAMPAIGN RHETORIC FOUND but LEFT NULL on public_justice q0 per the SKILL's sparse-data-trap discipline: his campaign Safety page states 'Our police officers are continually overworked with too many open positions on the force… I will continually advocate for funding, more staffing, and the right work environments for first responders'; he also accepted Norfolk Professional Fire Fighters IAFF Local 68 (firefighter union, not police union). That is campaign-platform self-claim shape — same as the Doyle precedent (refinements/natl-local-VA-2026-06-02-1441.json), where the SKILL discipline kept the only police-related cell null to avoid a misleading 100/100 score from a 1-cell denominator. Without a police-union endorsement OR a quoted anti-defund denunciation OR an independent secondary vector, scoring TRUE alone would inflate this freshman to 100/100 — left null and marked roster_verified accordingly. Note: Mar 4, 2025 council vote to create a Norfolk Police Victim Services Unit and to accept the Va. Firearms Violence Intervention and Prevention Grant are routine procedural votes and are not separately scored. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Progressive Voters Guide endorsement is soft adverse signal but not scoreable), biblical_marriage (any cell — review of 2025 council minutes for Feb 25, Mar 4, May 20, Jun 10, Jun 24, Jul 15 found no Pride proclamation, council resolution, or vote on LGBTQ matters during McGee's tenure; Norfolk's Pride Month page is city PR, not a council vote), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — his Education page critiques NPS accreditation rate and urges Council/School Board collaboration but does not advocate vouchers/ESAs/homeschool protections; no statement on parental notification or CRT-SOGI), christian_liberty (any cell — NO religious self-identification located in campaign bio, official city bio, or social pages; the 'Navy veteran/home preacher' claim found in one web search is a confused conflation with an unrelated Father Tony McGee — do NOT use), economic_stewardship q2 (he campaigned on property-tax-rate reduction calling Norfolk's rates 'a competitive disadvantage,' which is tax-cutting not BBA — not scored), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no statements on bail/restitution/public-health framing/Romans-13), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — took office Jan 1 2025, no documented opposition to state ESG/lockdown/mandate posture), border_immigration (any cell — Norfolk listed by CIS as sanctuary but no McGee statement located), self_defense q0/q1 (the 2020 Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance was pulled before any vote and never returned — no firearms vote during McGee's tenure; the Jan 28 2025 council action on the VA Firearms Violence Intervention and Prevention Grant was grant-acceptance, not a carry restriction, and is not scored). Counter-archetype context: business-friendly tax-cutting freshman with D-aligned endorsement slate AND affirmative campaign-platform pro-police-staffing advocacy.
